name: Mutrah Corniche
summary: A waterfront promenade framed by old merchant houses, frankincense stalls, and the harbor-side rhythm of Muscat.
description: Mutrah is one of Oman's easiest first-day experiences: wander the corniche, browse the souq, then climb toward the fort viewpoints as the light turns gold over the harbor.
practicalInfo: Arrive near sunset for cooler weather
Carry small cash for the souq
Dress modestly around traditional market areas
travelTips: Arrive near sunset for cooler weather
Carry small cash for the souq
Dress modestly around traditional market areas
seoDescription: A waterfront promenade framed by old merchant houses, frankincense stalls, and the harbor-side rhythm of Muscat.

name: Jebel Akhdar
summary: Terraced villages, canyon views, rose farms, and crisp mountain air high above central Oman.
description: Known as the Green Mountain, Jebel Akhdar rewards travelers with cooler temperatures, old stone villages, and balcony trails overlooking dramatic wadis.
practicalInfo: A 4WD is required at the mountain checkpoint
Bring a light layer for evenings
Book lunch stops ahead in peak season
travelTips: A 4WD is required at the mountain checkpoint
Bring a light layer for evenings
Book lunch stops ahead in peak season
seoDescription: Terraced villages, canyon views, rose farms, and crisp mountain air high above central Oman.

name: Wahiba Sands
summary: Rolling copper dunes, quiet camps, and star-heavy skies at the edge of Oman's eastern desert.
description: Wahiba Sands is ideal for a slow overnight: cross soft dunes at golden hour, share Omani coffee, and wake before sunrise when the desert is almost silent.
practicalInfo: Use an experienced local driver for dune routes
Confirm camp transfer details before arrival
travelTips: Use an experienced local driver for dune routes
Confirm camp transfer details before arrival
seoDescription: Rolling copper dunes, quiet camps, and star-heavy skies at the edge of Oman's eastern desert.

name: Salalah Khareef
summary: Mist, waterfalls, coconut palms, and green hills during Oman's remarkable monsoon season.
description: During Khareef, Dhofar feels completely different from northern Oman. Use Salalah as a base for wadis, viewpoints, beaches, and frankincense heritage sites.
practicalInfo: Roads can be foggy in the hills
travelTips: Roads can be foggy in the hills
seoDescription: Mist, waterfalls, coconut palms, and green hills during Oman's remarkable monsoon season.

name: Wadi Shab
summary: A canyon hike with turquoise pools, palm pockets, and a hidden cave swim near the coast road.
description: Wadi Shab blends a short boat crossing, rocky hiking, and swimming. It is one of Oman's best active day trips when water levels and conditions are suitable.
practicalInfo: Avoid visiting after heavy rain warnings
travelTips: Avoid visiting after heavy rain warnings
seoDescription: A canyon hike with turquoise pools, palm pockets, and a hidden cave swim near the coast road.

name: Nizwa Fort
summary: A monumental circular tower, old souq lanes, and a gateway into Oman's interior heritage routes.
description: Nizwa Fort anchors a rewarding heritage day. Pair the fort with the souq, nearby villages, and mountain drives into Al Dakhiliyah.
practicalInfo: Combine with Bahla or Jabrin for a full heritage loop
travelTips: Combine with Bahla or Jabrin for a full heritage loop
seoDescription: A monumental circular tower, old souq lanes, and a gateway into Oman's interior heritage routes.
